title: Neurological Selfless Rewiring
definition: The physical rewiring of the brain that occurs as a result of intentional mental training, leading to a reduced emphasis on self-referential thought and increased decentralized processing.

difficulty_levels.beginner: Through meditation and mindfulness practices, our brains can change in ways that reduce the constant hum of thoughts about ourselves. This shift makes us less self-centered and more focused on the present moment.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: The long-term impact of intentional mental training leads to changes in brain structure and function, particularly in the default mode network, salience network, amyggdala, and prefrontal cortex. These changes result in reduced self-referential processing and a more integrated, bottom-up approach to perception.
difficulty_levels.expert: The practice of intentional mental training creates novel connections between brain regions that alter the functioning of default networks, salience networks, amyggdala, and prefrontal cortex. This rewiring results in reduced personal bias, less threat response, and increased global synchrony and connectivity, ultimately leading to a profound physical transformation that eliminates the need for a self-centered perspective.

extra_fields.insight: Intentional mental training has a profound impact on the brain's structure and function, altering the way we perceive and process information.
